ops.title: Fix malformed webstorage origin from LevelDB key parsing
ops.description: Repro (2026-07-13, this Mac): cookiesync cookies --format webstorage -- github.com | jq -r '.origins[].origin' prints a second origin 'https://github.com/^0https://lemire.me'. Likely the webstorage LevelDB key parser (internal/cookie/webstorage.go) not splitting a namespace/map key on the ^0 separator, gluing two origins. Impact: abwc-seed (cc-skills) opens the malformed origin as a URL during storage seeding — harmless page load, wrong scoping; 1 of 1,484 github.com entries lands on the wrong page.

ops.text: cookiesync cookies --format webstorage -- github.com emits only well-formed origins (today it emits 'https://github.com/^0https://lemire.me' — two origins glued by an unparsed ^0 separator)
a regression test covers the offending LevelDB key shape
